title: Understand: radioactive decay
description: Check your understanding of radioactive decay in this set of free practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
Complete the statement.

**An alpha particle can also be described as a $\_\_\_\_\_\_.$**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**An alpha particle is composed of what combination of particles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Complete the statement.**

An alpha particle is composed of [[☃ dropdown 1]] protons and [[☃ dropdown 2]] neutrons.
hints: An alpha particle is composed of four subatomic particles.
Two of these particles are protons, and two of them are neutrons.
An alpha particle is composed of 2 protons and 2 neutrons.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the table to show the particles emitted during beta minus and beta plus decay.**

| Charged particle | Neutral particle
:- | :-: | :-:
beta minus decay | [[☃ dropdown 1]] | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
beta plus decay | [[☃ dropdown 3]] | [[☃ dropdown 4]]
hints: Beta minus decay produces a negatively charged particle of matter and a neutral particle of antimatter. So, beta minus decay produces an electron and an antineutrino.
Beta plus decay produces a positively charged particle of antimatter and a neutral particle of matter. So, beta plus decay produces a positron and a neutrino.

The correct answer is:

| Charged particle | Neutral particle
:- | :-: | :-:
beta minus decay | electron | antineutrino
beta plus decay | positron | neutrino

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statements.**

During beta minus decay, a nucleus emits a(n) [[☃ dropdown 1]] and a(n) [[☃ dropdown 2]].

During beta plus decay, a nucleus emits a(n) [[☃ dropdown 3]] and a(n) [[☃ dropdown 4]].
hints: Beta minus decay produces a negatively charged particle of matter and a neutral particle of antimatter. So, beta minus decay produces an electron and an antineutrino.
Beta plus decay produces a positively charged particle of antimatter and a neutral particle of matter. So, beta plus decay produces a positron and a neutrino.

The correct statements are:

During beta minus decay, a nucleus emits an electron and an antineutrino.

During beta plus decay, a nucleus emits a positron and a neutrino.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statements.**

In beta plus decay, a nucleus emits a(n) [[☃ dropdown 7]] and a(n) [[☃ dropdown 8]].

In beta minus decay, a nucleus emits a(n) [[☃ dropdown 9]] and a(n) [[☃ dropdown 10]].
hints: Beta plus decay produces a positively charged particle of antimatter and a neutral particle of matter. So, beta plus decay produces a positron and a neutrino.
Beta minus decay produces a negatively charged particle of matter and a neutral particle of antimatter. So, beta minus decay produces an electron and an antineutrino.

The correct statements are:

In beta plus decay, a nucleus emits a positron and a neutrino.

In beta minus decay, a nucleus emits an electron and an antineutrino.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens in a nucleus during beta minus decay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A proton turns into a neutron.
A neutron turns into a proton.
Two protons and two neutrons are ejected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens in a nucleus during beta plus decay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A proton turns into a neutron.
A neutron turns into a proton.
Two protons and two neutrons are ejected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which type of radioactive decay does a neutron turn into a proton?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which statement is true about gamma radiation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It often accompanies alpha and beta decay.
It has the lowest penetrating power of any ionizing radiation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement is true about gamma radiation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It always occurs independently of alpha and beta decay.
It has the lowest penetrating power of any ionizing radiation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement is true about gamma radiation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It always occurs independently of alpha and beta decay.
It has the highest penetrating power of any ionizing radiation.
